message:
feat(routing): add URL generation and reverse routing system

Implement comprehensive URL generation for named routes, enabling type-safe
reverse routing similar to Flask's url_for() or Django's reverse().

UrlRegistry provides:
- Route name registration with pattern storage
- Path parameter substitution with validation
- Query parameter encoding with proper URL escaping
- Root path support for reverse proxy deployments

UrlError enum handles:
- RouteNotFound: Unknown route name lookup
- MissingParam: Required path parameter not provided
- InvalidParam: Value doesn't match converter type (e.g., non-int for :int)

Example usage:
  registry.register("get_user", "/users/{id}");
  registry.url_for("get_user", &[("id", "42")], &[("fields", "name")])
  // Returns: "/users/42?fields=name"

Also adds SSE (Server-Sent Events) module for streaming responses.
Syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-bss","title":"Dependency caching with request scope","description":"Implement request-scoped dependency caching:

BEHAVIOR (per FastAPI spec):
- use_cache: bool = true (default)
- Cache key: (callable, sorted_scopes, computed_scope)
- Same dependency called twice = same instance

CACHE LOCATIONS:
- 'request' scope: Cached for entire request
- 'function' scope: Fresh each call (no caching)

STORAGE:
- Store in RequestContext or dedicated DependencyCache
- TypeId-based lookup for type safety
- Clear on request completion

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- use_cache=true returns same instance
- use_cache=false returns fresh instance
- Scope rules enforced
- No memory leaks

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Caching behavior
- Unit: Cache key generation
- Unit: Scope isolation
- Integration: Complex dependency graphs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:11:08.615093525Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:01:56.227231922Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:01:56.226790510Z","close_reason":"Already fully implemented - DependencyCache, DependencyScope, DependsConfig, NoCache all in place with comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["caching","di","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-bss","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-aha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-c75","title":"Parameter alias support","description":"Implement parameter aliasing:

ALIAS TYPES (per FastAPI):
- alias: Alternative name in request
- validation_alias: Name for validation
- serialization_alias: Name for serialization

HEADER EXAMPLE:
#[param(alias = 'X-Custom-Token')]
token: String
// Looks for header 'X-Custom-Token' instead of 'token'

QUERY EXAMPLE:
#[param(alias = 'q')]
query: String
// Accepts ?q=value instead of ?query=value

ALIAS PROPAGATION:
if alias is set and serialization_alias is None:
    serialization_alias = alias
if alias is set and validation_alias is None:
    validation_alias = alias

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Alias used for extraction
- Alias appears in OpenAPI
- Propagation rules work
- Works with all param types

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Header alias
- Unit: Query alias
- Unit: Propagation rules
- OpenAPI: Alias in schema","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:51.872134286Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:57:02.052808567Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:57:02.052743396Z","close_reason":"Implemented parameter alias support: alias, validation_alias, serialization_alias fields in ParamAttrs and ParamMeta. Added propagation logic and OpenAPI integration. All tests pass (35 param_meta tests, 30 macro tests).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alias","params","phase-2"]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-cad","title":"Lifespan context manager for async startup/shutdown","description":"Implement lifespan async context manager (preferred over on_startup/on_shutdown). Usage: async context manager that yields after startup, cleanup runs on shutdown. Allows shared state between startup/shutdown (e.g. database connections). Pattern: @asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(app): startup(); yield {state}; shutdown(). App state available to handlers. Test: verify startup runs before accepting, shutdown after drain, state accessible in handlers, replaces deprecated on_startup/on_shutdown when provided.","notes":"Implemented LifespanError, LifespanScope<T>, BoxLifespanFn types. Added AppBuilder.lifespan() method. Lifespan runs before regular startup hooks, cleanup runs after shutdown hooks. State injected via insert_any. Exports added to lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:43:53.160151380Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:15:56.661340451Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:15:56.661246063Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","async","lifecycle","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-cad","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rbm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-e5i","title":"Request/response logging middleware","description":"Implement middleware for automatic request/response logging:
- Log request: method, path, headers (filtered), body size
- Log response: status, headers, body size, duration
- Configurable header filtering (hide Authorization, etc)
- Body logging with size limits (optional)
- Correlation with request_id

DEPENDS ON: Logging infrastructure, Middleware trait

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every request/response automatically logged
- Sensitive headers redacted by default
- Duration accurate to microsecond
- Body truncation works correctly

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Header filtering logic
- Unit: Body truncation
- Integration: Full request/response cycle logging
- Security: Sensitive data not leaked","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:38:56.807237379Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.330456583Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T15:43:51.703245998Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","middleware","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-e5i","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-6vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-e5i","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-fac","title":"Connection draining on shutdown","description":"Implement graceful connection draining:
- Stop accepting new connections on shutdown signal
- Wait for in-flight requests to complete (with timeout)
- Force-close connections after drain timeout
- Integration with asupersync region cancellation

DEPENDS ON: Graceful shutdown, TCP server

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- In-flight requests complete during shutdown
- New connections rejected during drain
- Drain timeout forces closure
- Clean exit with no resource leaks

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Integration: Shutdown during active requests
- Integration: Drain timeout behavior
- Unit: Signal handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:45.332377934Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:38.952857482Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:38.952790225Z","close_reason":"Implemented connection draining on shutdown:
- Added drain_timeout config (default 30s)
- Added draining AtomicBool flag to TcpServer
- Added is_draining(), start_drain(), wait_for_drain(), drain() methods
- Accept loop checks draining flag and returns ServerError::Shutdown
- wait_for_drain polls connection count with configurable interval
- drain() combines start_drain + wait_for_drain with configured timeout
- Added 11 unit tests for drain functionality
- Total test count: 210 passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["drain","phase-1","server","shutdown"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-fac","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-n12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-fhsv","title":"Cookbook with common patterns and recipes","description":"Collection of copy-paste solutions for common tasks. Practical, problem-focused documentation.

Recipes:

Each Recipe:

Acceptance Criteria:

Tests:
- All recipe code compiles
- Examples are tested","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:17:19.931835596Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:17:19.931835596Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cookbook","documentation","patterns"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-fhsv","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-x3o3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-fjy","title":"Request body size limits for DoS prevention","description":"Enforce maximum request body size to prevent denial-of-service via large payloads. Apply at parser level before buffering.

**Implementation:**
- Global default limit (configurable, default 1MB)
- Per-route override (for file uploads)
- Early rejection (before full body read)
- Content-Length validation
- Chunked body limit enforcement
- 413 Payload Too Large response

**Configuration:**
- default_max_body_size: usize
- per_route max_body_size annotation
- streaming threshold (when to switch to streaming)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Bodies over limit rejected with 413
- Rejection happens early, not after buffering
- Per-route overrides work
- Chunked bodies also limited
- File upload routes can have higher limits

**Tests:**
- 1MB body on default route returns 413
- 100KB body on default route succeeds
- File upload route accepts 10MB body
- Chunked body over limit returns 413
- Content-Length header validated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:59.925909405Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:19:58.511526909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:19:58.511462840Z","close_reason":"Implemented request body size limits for DoS prevention. Added BodyLimitConfig to RequestContext, wired Json extractor to use configurable limits, added comprehensive tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","limits","phase-1","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-fjy","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-2l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
